H1.description: Prophage-proximal AMR genes are disproportionately accessory
H1.notes: Overall effect significant but per-species results heterogeneous (33/74 show OR>1)

H2.description: Species with higher prophage burden carry broader AMR repertoires
H2.notes: Robust across all 5 major phyla, persists after controlling for genome count

H3.description: Prophage-proximal AMR genes show different fitness signatures
H3.notes: Insufficient overlap between fitness browser organisms and AMR analysis species

key_findings: 55.7% of AMR gene instances share contigs with strict prophage markers
10.4% of AMR genes are within 10 genes of a prophage marker
Prophage density explains 30% of variance in AMR breadth across 4,770 species
Association robust: partial rho=0.46 after controlling for genome count
Physical proximity effect modest (OR=1.10) but consistent at broader thresholds
limitations: Gene position from gene_id parsing (ordinal, not bp distance)
Prophage markers from keyword/Pfam matching, not dedicated prophage prediction
20 genomes sampled per species for co-localization (not exhaustive)
Core/accessory labels species-specific; same gene may differ across species
Fitness browser covers only 48 organisms; H3 not fully testable
